note: Master tool catalogue (equipment, not consumed by a build — reused across projects). Every tool exists once. Projects reference these by toolId. Prices in INR.

T0001.name: Temperature-controlled soldering iron
T0001.alternatives: Any temp-controlled iron 40-80W works. A fixed-temp basic iron is usable but riskier near the ADuM isolator's small pins.
T0001.notes: Core tool for every build in this repo.

T0002.name: Lead-free solder wire
T0002.alternatives: 60/40 leaded solder is easier to work with if ventilation is good — wash hands after.

T0003.name: Digital multimeter
T0003.alternatives: Any auto-ranging DMM with a continuity beeper works.
T0003.notes: Used for harness voltage checks and continuity before every AC/mains connection.

T0006.name: Crimping tool (JST/Dupont)
T0006.alternatives: Pre-crimped jumper wires avoid needing this, but a spare header/connector repair always needs it eventually.

T0008.name: Hot glue gun
T0008.notes: Strain relief / securing loose harnesses inside enclosures.

T0009.name: Heat shrink tubing (assorted)
T0009.alternatives: Electrical tape in a pinch — not recommended near the non-isolated S403 side.

T0010.name: Heat gun (mini)
T0010.alternatives: A lighter works in a pinch but scorches easily — go slow and keep distance.

T0011.name: Precision screwdriver set
T0011.notes: For AC casing, ceiling fixtures, enclosure lids.

T0012.name: ESD wrist strap
T0012.alternatives: Touch a grounded metal object periodically if unavailable — not as reliable.
T0012.notes: Recommended before handling the ADuM1201 / bare ESP32 boards.

T0014.name: Safety glasses
T0014.notes: For mains-side wiring (ceiling bulbs, AC casing) and cutting/drilling.
